core_concept: World Geography: Canada and Important Rivers
facts: Canada is the second largest country in the world by area, located directly north of the United States, and while it has many rivers, the Amazon River is in South America.
The Nile River is the longest river in the world, stretching over 6,600 kilometers through northeastern Africa.
The Mississippi River in the United States helped many settlements grow because people could travel and transport goods along it.
The Ganges River in India flows from the Himalayas and provides water for farming to millions of people.
misconceptions: Students often think the Amazon River is the longest river in the world because it is very wide and carries a lot of water, but actually the Nile is longer.
Students may believe that Canada is a small country because they only see a small part of it on maps that focus on the United States, but Canada is actually the second largest country by area.
